title: Fluffy Fermented Ethiopian Sirloin Stew
description: A rich, slowcooked stew featuring tender sirloin, fermented tomato paste, and a fluffy yogurt finish, perfect for a lazy Sunday.
instructions: Season the cubed sirloin with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium‑high heat; sear the steak cubes until browned on all sides, then remove and set aside.
Add diced onion to the same pot and sauté until translucent.
Stir in minced garlic, grated ginger, and berbere spice; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add fermented tomato paste and diced tomatoes; cook for 2 minutes.
Sprinkle flour over the mixture, stir to coat, and cook 1 minute to remove raw flour taste.
Add vegetable stock, bring to a simmer, return the seared steak cubes, cover, and slow cook at low heat (60–70 °C) for 60 minutes until the meat is tender.
Remove the pot from heat, whisk in low‑fat yogurt to create a fluffy, airy consistency; stir until integrated, season with lemon juice, garnish with chopped cilantro, and serve hot.
